modules.title: Introduction to Operating Systems
topics: Function, Evolution, Different types of Operating Systems, Desirable Characteristics and features of an OS
Operating Systems Services – Types of Services, Different ways of providing these services (Commands, System Calls)
Need of System Calls, Low‑level implementation of System Calls, Portability issues

modules.title: File Systems (Secondary Storage Management)
topics: File concept and the view of users versus system programmers
Hard‑disk organization, disk formatting and file‑system creation
Disk‑space allocation methods: contiguous, linked, indexed
Disk partitioning and mounting; directory structures; file protection
Case studies of file systems used in Unix/Linux and Windows
System calls used for file management

modules.title: Process Management
topics: Concept of a process and the process‑state diagram
Different types of schedulers and CPU‑scheduling algorithms; evaluation of scheduling algorithms
Threads – user‑level and kernel‑level, thread scheduling, multiprocessor/multicore scheduling
Case studies of process management in Unix/Linux and Windows; related system calls

Concurrency and synchronization – real/virtual concurrency, mutual exclusion, critical‑section problem, mutex locks, monitors, semaphores, WAIT/SIGNAL operations, classic synchronization problems, inter‑process communication

modules.title: Memory Management
topics: Memory‑management techniques – contiguous allocation; non‑contiguous allocation (paging, segmentation, paged segmentation); comparative analysis
Virtual memory – concept, overlay, dynamic linking and loading, implementation by demand paging
Memory management approaches in Unix/Linux and Windows

modules.title: Input/Output Management
topics: I/O system models – program‑controlled, interrupt‑driven, concurrent I/O; synchronous/asynchronous and blocking/non‑blocking operations; I/O buffering; application I/O interface; kernel I/O subsystem; translation of I/O requests to hardware operations
Protection and security issues and mechanisms
Introduction to multiprocessor, real‑time, embedded and mobile operating systems

reference_books: Andrew S. Tanenbaum, “Modern Operating Systems”, Prentice Hall.
Robert Love, “Linux Kernel Development”, Pearson.
Maurice J. Bach, “The Design of Unix Operating System”, Pearson.
Bovet & Cesati, “Understanding the Linux Kernel”, O'Reilly.

modules.title: DBMS Concepts and Architecture
topics: Introduction to DBMS concepts and architecture
Database approach vs. traditional file accessing approach
Entity‑Relationship (ER) data model: entities, attributes, entity types
Generalization, aggregation and specialization in ER modeling
Transforming ER diagram into relational tables
Other data models: object‑oriented, network, relational
Hashing and indexing structures: single‑level and multilevel indices

modules.title: Relational Data Models
topics: Relational data model fundamentals: domains, tuples, attributes, relations
Characteristics of relations and keys (primary, candidate, foreign)
Relational database schemas and integrity constraints
SQL Data Definition Language (DDL) and Data Manipulation Language (DML)
Complex SQL queries and various join operations
Indexing and triggers in relational databases
Relational algebra operations: select, project, join, division, outer union
Relational calculus: tuple‑oriented and domain‑oriented forms

modules.title: Database Design and Query Optimization
topics: Algorithms for implementing select, project, and join operations

modules.title: Transaction Processing and Concurrency Control
topics: Serializability testing: conflict‑serializable and view‑serializable schedules
Recoverability and recovery from transaction failures
Concurrency control techniques: locking protocols, timestamp protocols, validation protocols
Multiple‑granularity locking and multiversion concurrency control
Data mining and data warehousing basics
Comparative study of OODBMS and DBMS
Temporal, deductive, multimedia, web and mobile databases

modules.title: Case Study of Relational DBMS (Oracle/PostgreSQL/MySQL)
topics: Architecture of major RDBMS (Oracle, PostgreSQL, MySQL)
Physical file organization and memory structures
Data dictionary and dynamic performance views
Security, role management, privilege management, profiles
Advanced SQL queries: hierarchical, inline, flashback
Cursor management: nested and parameterized cursors
Stored procedures and usage of parameters
Triggers, mutating table errors, instead‑of triggers

reference_books: Rob, “Database System: Design Implementation & Management”, Cengage Learning.
Atul Kahate, “Introduction to Database Management System”, Pearson Education.
Oracle 9i Database Administration Fundamental-I, Volume I, Oracle Press, TMH.
Paneerselvam, “Database Management System”, PHI Learning.

modules.title: Introduction
topics: Impact of the Web on IR
Role of artificial intelligence (AI) in IR

modules.title: Boolean and Vector Space Retrieval Models
Web Search Overview
topics: Web structure and user‑paid placement search‑engine optimization

modules.title: Link Analysis
topics: Relevance scoring and ranking for the Web
Collaborative filtering and content‑based recommendation of documents and products

modules.title: Information Filtering
topics: Information filtering: organization and relevance feedback
Categorization algorithms: naive Bayes, decision trees, nearest‑neighbor
Clustering algorithms: agglomerative clustering, k‑means, expectation‑maximization (EM)

reference_books: C. Manning, P. Raghvan and H. Schütze: Introduction to Information Retrieval, Cambridge University Press.
Ricardo Baeza‑Yates and Berthier Ribeiro‑Neto, Modern Information Retrieval: The Concepts and Technology behind Search, ACM Press Books.
Bruce Croft, Donald Metzler and Trevor Strohman, Search Engines: Information Retrieval in Practice, Addison‑Wesley.
Mark Levene, An Introduction to Search Engines and Web Navigation, Wiley.

modules.title: Introduction
topics: Introduction and History of Deep Learning
Dimensionality reduction – Principal Component Analysis (PCA) and Singular Value Decomposition (SVD)

modules.title: Convolutional Neural Networks
topics: Introduction to Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) and architectures
Convolutional terminology – ReLU activation, stride, padding, pooling, convolution operations
Canonical CNN models – LeNet, AlexNet, ZF‑Net, VGGNet, GoogLeNet, ResNet, R‑CNN
Deep Dream and Deep Art applications
Regularization techniques – dropout, drop‑connect, unit pruning, stochastic pooling, artificial data injection, early stopping, weight decay, limiting the number of parameters

modules.title: Recurrent Neural Networks
topics: Introduction to deep Recurrent Neural Networks (RNN) and architectures
Attention mechanisms – standard attention, visual attention, hierarchical attention
Applications of deep RNNs in image processing, natural language processing, speech recognition, and video analytics

modules.title: Generative Models
topics: Restricted Boltzmann Machines (RBMs) and Gibbs sampling
Applications of deep generative models in object detection, speech and image recognition, video analysis, NLP, and medical science

reference_books: Aurelien Geon, “Hands‑On Machine Learning with Scikit‑Learn and Tensorflow: Concepts, Tools, and Techniques to Build Intelligent Systems”, O'Reilly.
Andreas Müller, "Introduction to Machine Learning with Python: A Guide for Data Scientists", O'Reilly.
Adam Gibson and Josh Patterson, "Deep Learning: A Practitioner's Approach", O'Reilly.

modules.title: Introduction
topics: Basic Calculus for optimization: Limits and multivariate functions
Derivatives and linear approximations: Single‑variate functions and multivariate functions

modules.title: Responsible Machine Learning
topics: Positive feedback loops and negative feedback loops

modules.title: Machine Learning in Production and Planning
topics: Time and space complexity in production
When to retain the model? Logging ML model versioning

modules.title: Care and Feeding of Your Machine Learning Model

reference_books: Jeeva Jose, Introduction to Machine Learning, Khanna Book Publishing, 2020.
Rajiv Chopra, Machine Learning, Khanna Book Publishing, 2021.
Optimization for Machine Learning, Suvrit Sra, Sebastian Nowozin and Stephen J. Wright, MIT Press, 2011.
Optimization in Machine Learning and Applications, Suresh Chandra Satapathy, Anand J. Kulkarni, Springer, 2019.

modules.title: Disease detection with computer vision
topics: Building and training a model for medical diagnosis
Image classification and class imbalance handling
Generating additional samples for training (data augmentation)

modules.title: Evaluating models
topics: Sensitivity and specificity as evaluation metrics
MRI data acquisition and image registration
Loss functions specific to segmentation tasks
Assessment across different populations and diagnostic technologies

modules.title: Linear prognostic models
topics: Techniques to mitigate overfitting in trees
Missing data handling in prognostic datasets

modules.title: Medical Treatment Effect Estimation
topics: Analysis of randomized controlled trial data

reference_books: https://www.coursera.org/learn/ai-for-medical-diagnosis
https://www.coursera.org/learn/ai-for-medicalprognosis#syllabus
https://www.coursera.org/learn/ai-for-medical-treatment#syllabus
Deep Medicine: How Artificial Intelligence Can Make Healthcare Human Again, Eric Topol, Basic Books, 1st edition 2019.
Machine Learning and AI for Healthcare: Big Data for Improved Health Outcomes, Arjun Panesar, Apress, 1st ed. Edition, 2019.
Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, 2020, ISBN 978-012-818438-7, Elsevier Inc

modules.title: Introduction
topics: Language Modeling: Grammar‑based LM, Statistical LM

modules.title: Semantics and Pragmatics
topics: Word senses and relations between senses
Word‑sense disambiguation (supervised, dictionary & thesaurus, bootstrapping methods)
Word similarity using thesaurus and distributional methods

reference_books: Breck Baldwin, Language Processing with Java and LingPipe Cookbook, Atlantic Publisher
Richard M Reese, Natural Language Processing with Java, O'Reilly Media
Nitin Indurkhya and Fred J. Damerau, Handbook of Natural Language Processing, Chapman and Hall/CRC Press
Tanveer Siddiqui, U.S. Tiwary, Natural Language Processing and Information Retrieval, Oxford University Press

modules.title: Introduction to Computational Intelligence (CI)
topics: Decision Trees: introduction, evaluation, splitting criteria, implementation
Neural Networks: introduction, types, issues, implementation, applications

modules.title: Fuzzy Set Theory
topics: Definitions and terminology of fuzzy sets
Fuzzy inference systems and input‑space partitioning

modules.title: Rough Set Theory
topics: Rough set fundamentals and knowledge representation
Set approximations, accuracy, vagueness, and uncertainty
Graphical models, variable elimination, belief propagation

modules.title: Evolutionary Computation
topics: Genetic Algorithms: basic genetics, concepts, working principle
Chromosome encoding, fitness functions, selection methods
Genetic programming, evolutionary programming, evolutionary strategies

modules.title: Swarm Intelligence
topics: Ant Colony Optimization (ACO): overview and algorithm
Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO): basics, social network structure, parameters, algorithm

reference_books: Russell C. Eberhart and Yuhui Shi, Computational Intelligence: Concepts to Implementations, Morgan Kaufmann Publishers.
Andries P. Engelbrecht, Computational Intelligence: An Introduction, Wiley Publishing.
David E. Goldberg, Genetic Algorithm in Search Optimization and Machine Learning, Pearson Education.
Jagdish Chand Bansal, Pramod Kumar Singh, Nikhil R. Pal, Evolutionary and Swarm Intelligence Algorithms, Springer Publishing.
S. Rajasekaran, G.A. Vijayalakshmi Pai, “Neural Networks, Fuzzy Logic, Genetic Algorithms Synthesis and Applications”, PHI.
Timothy J. Ross, Fuzzy Logic with Engineering Applications, McGraw‑Hill.
Simon Haykin, Neural Networks: A Comprehensive Foundation, Prentice Hall.
